license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7kvhkw6
title: Chloroform Phenol Phage genome isolation
author: Marijn Ceelen

steps_raw.instruction: Use cleaned phage stock from the phage stock protocol. Add 50 µL DNase I 10x buffer, 1 µL DNase I (1 U/µL), and 1 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL) for 01:30:00 at 37 °C without shaking to remove E. coli DNA and RNA.
Add 20 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA (final concentration 20 Molarity (M) ) and incubate for 00:15:00 at 75 °C to deactivate DNase I and RNase A
Add 2 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL) and 50 µL SDS 10% to digest the phage capsid and incubate overnight at 56 °C without shaking.
Add equal volume phenol/chloroform/iosamyl alcohol (25:24:1) and mix well (do not vortex, as genome is easily damaged)
Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g
Add equal volume chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1) and mix well (do not vortex, as genome is easily damaged)
Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g
Add 1/10 volume of Sodium acetate 3 Molarity (M)
Dry pellet. Heating to 50 °C c increases drying.
